Home / Research Projects / safe-evidence-redactor: Privacy-First Redaction Library and MCP Server

safe-evidence-redactor: Privacy-First Redaction Library and MCP Server

Defensive AI tooling for offline PII redaction in security reports, logs, and AI outputs, exposed as a library, CLI, and MCP server.

PrivacyDefensive AIPII RedactionMCP
View code

Overview

Library, CLI, and MCP server that remove sensitive evidence from text and JSON while preserving enough context for debugging. Walks nested JSON, redacts only values, and reports JSON paths. Supports three modes (minimal, balanced, strict) and category-, domain-, and field-level allowlists. Fully offline with zero telemetry. Used by phish-triage-mcp for stronger PII redaction.

Methods

Pattern-based redaction with category allowlists, JSON path tracking, MCP integration.

Evaluation

Internal pattern-coverage benchmarks; integrated and validated by phish-triage-mcp.

Key Contributions

  • Redaction categories: AUTH_HEADER, BEARER, JWT, API_KEY, EMAIL, UPI, PII, IPs.
  • Three redaction modes (minimal / balanced / strict) with allowlists.
  • JSON walker that preserves structure and reports redacted paths.
  • CLI (safe-redact) and MCP server (safe-redact-mcp) over stdio.
  • Fully offline, no telemetry, no network calls at runtime.

Research & Engineering Challenges

  • Designing redaction that preserves debuggability without leaking secrets.
  • Building a JSON walker reporting stable paths through nested arrays/objects.
  • Curating Indian-context categories alongside global vendor API key shapes.

Tech Stack

TypeScript Node.js MCP SDK GitHub Packages

Status

Released: v0.1.2

Author
